title: Ethnicity, Insurgency, and Civil War
authors: Fearon, James D.; Laitin, David D.
abstract: Using a new data set on civil wars, 1945-1999, we challenge the ethnic and religious conflict explanation. We find that neither ethnic nor religious diversity is associated with higher risk of civil war. Instead, conditions that favor insurgency — poverty, political instability, rough terrain, and large populations — are the strongest predictors.
